questions.question: Which of the following is NOT covered by COSHH because it has its own separate regulations?
options: Disinfectant used on a commode
Thick bleach
Dust from a dry mop
Asbestos

questions.question: An effect that appears quickly after exposure, sometimes within minutes, such as stinging eyes, dizziness or a chemical burn, is described as which type of effect?
options: Delayed
Cumulative
Acute
Chronic

questions.question: A chronic health effect from a hazardous substance is best described as one that:
options: Only ever affects the skin
Appears within minutes of a single exposure
Is always too trivial to worry about
Builds up slowly over months or years of repeated contact

questions.question: In care work, dermatitis of the hands is most often caused by:
options: Eating contaminated food
Frequent contact with water, cleaning products and long spells in gloves ('wet work')
Breathing in fine dust
A single one-off chemical splash

questions.question: Once a worker's airways have become sensitised in occupational asthma, what is true?
options: Even tiny amounts of the substance can trigger an attack
They can no longer be affected by that substance
The condition always clears up on its own
Only very large amounts will cause any symptoms

questions.question: Under the CLP Regulation, what does a hazard pictogram look like?
options: A red-bordered diamond with a black symbol on a white background
A plain yellow triangle
A solid green circle
A blue square with white text

questions.question: Which of these is a signal word you would find on a CLP hazard label?
options: Emergency
Hazardous
Toxic-alert
Danger

questions.question: A safety data sheet is a standard document made up of how many sections?
What is the key difference between a safety data sheet (SDS) and a COSHH assessment?
options: They are simply two names for exactly the same document
The SDS is written by your own employer for each task
The COSHH assessment is written by the product's manufacturer
The SDS is supplier information about the substance, while the COSHH assessment is your organisation's plan for using it safely in your setting

questions.question: Where do Legionella bacteria multiply, creating a risk of serious pneumonia if fine droplets are breathed in?
options: In warm, stagnant water
In dry household dust
Inside sealed bleach bottles
In cold, fast-running water

questions.question: Which everyday action helps keep the risk of Legionella under control?
options: Running taps and showers regularly and reporting water faults
Leaving rarely used showers switched off for months
Storing water at a warm room temperature
Mixing bleach with a descaler to clean the shower

questions.question: What is the golden rule about decanting (transferring) a chemical from its original container?
options: Only decant into clear glass jars
Always decant into a clean squash bottle for convenience
Never decant into an unlabelled container, and never into a drinks bottle or food tub
Decanting is fine as long as you remember what is inside

questions.question: A hazardous chemical has splashed into a person's eyes. What is the correct first response?
options: Wait to see whether the stinging settles by itself
Rinse gently with clean water for at least ten to fifteen minutes and get medical help
Apply a soothing skin cream to the eyes
Rub the eyes with a dry cloth

questions.question: If someone has swallowed a hazardous substance, what should you do?
options: Do nothing and keep them at home
Always make them vomit straight away
Do not make them vomit unless the safety data sheet says so, and ring 111 or 999
Give them a large drink of milk and wait

questions.question: COSHH does not cover asbestos, lead or radioactive substances, which have their own separate regulations.
options: True
False

questions.question: A safety data sheet and a COSHH assessment are the same document with two different names.
options: True
False

questions.question: Good ventilation, such as opening a window or using extraction, lowers the concentration of fumes and vapours in the air.
options: True
False

questions.question: A tight-fitting respirator (RPE) will still seal properly and protect the wearer over a beard.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are safe storage rules for hazardous substances under COSHH? (Select all that apply)
options: Stockpile as much as possible in case you run out
Keep them in a cool, dry, well-ventilated place
Store containers upright so they cannot leak
Store bleach next to acids to save cupboard space
Store them away from food and out of reach of the people you support

questions.question: Which groups of people are especially vulnerable to hazardous substances? (Select all that apply)
options: Anyone who is pregnant or breastfeeding
New and young workers
People who never come into contact with any cleaning products
Those with existing skin or breathing conditions

questions.question: Which of the following are correct first-response actions when someone is exposed to a hazardous substance? (Select all that apply)
options: Always make the person vomit whatever they have been exposed to
If it has been breathed in, move the person to fresh air
Keep the product label or safety data sheet with you so medical staff know what they are dealing with
For skin contact, remove contaminated clothing and rinse the area with plenty of water
Ignore it completely if the symptoms seem mild

questions.question: Which of the following are correct ways to dispose of hazardous waste in a care setting? (Select all that apply)
options: Put clinical waste in the correct colour-coded bags
Dispose of waste as instructed by your procedures
Pour leftover hazardous chemicals down the drain
Put sharps in a sharps bin

questions.question: COSHH stands for the Control of Substances Hazardous to ___ Regulations 2002.
An inflamed, cracked or blistered skin condition, usually on the hands, caused by frequent contact with water and cleaning products, is called ___.

Certain work-related illnesses caused by hazardous substances must be reported to the Health and Safety Executive under ___, the Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013.
